title: Supreme Court Declares Right to Walk on Safe Footpaths a Fundamental Right
summary: The Supreme Court of India ruled that the right to walk on footpaths is a fundamental right, derived from the right to life under Article 21 of the Constitution. The Court observed that roads without footpaths violate citizens' fundamental rights and directed authorities to ensure safe pedestrian infrastructure on all roads. The bench also called for enactment of a dedicated law to protect pedestrians and ensure safe walkways across the country.
exam_angle: Connects directly to the expansive interpretation of Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty) by the Supreme Court; relevant for questions on judicial activism and expansion of fundamental rights.
key_facts: Article invoked: Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty)
Court: Supreme Court of India
Direction: Footpaths to be provided on all roads; law to be enacted for pedestrian safety
Significance: Expands scope of Article 21 to include right to safe pedestrian infrastructure
source: The Indian Express / India Today / Business Standard

title: US-Iran Nuclear Talks: Next Round Scheduled in Switzerland Amid Delays
summary: The United States and Iran have agreed to hold the next round of nuclear talks in Switzerland, with Trump's special envoy and Iran's Foreign Minister set to participate. The talks faced delays due to intensifying Israel-Hezbollah clashes in West Asia. The negotiations are aimed at reviving a framework agreement to limit Iran's nuclear programme in exchange for sanctions relief.
exam_angle: Relevant for questions on Iran's nuclear programme, West Asia geopolitics, and India's energy security interests given India's historical oil imports from Iran.
key_facts: Venue of next talks: Switzerland
Parties: USA and Iran
Context: Ongoing Israel-Hezbollah conflict complicating diplomatic schedule
Objective: Framework agreement limiting Iran's nuclear activities for sanctions relief

title: Centre Assures Indian Farmers Will Be Fully Protected in India-US Trade Deal Negotiations
summary: The Indian government reassured that Indian farmers will be fully protected during ongoing India-US bilateral trade deal negotiations. Concerns had been raised that American agricultural exports, particularly subsidised farm produce, could undermine Indian farmers if import duties are reduced significantly. The government stated that agriculture will remain a sensitive sector and commitments will not jeopardise domestic food security.
exam_angle: Relevant for questions on India-US trade relations, WTO commitments, agricultural subsidies, and food security; connects to GS Paper 3 themes of agriculture and trade policy.
key_facts: Negotiation: India-US Bilateral Trade Agreement (BTA)
Key concern: Protection of Indian farmers from cheaper US agricultural imports
Government stance: Agriculture designated as a sensitive sector
Broader context: India-US tariff negotiations ongoing under Trump administration

title: Japan PM Sanae Takaichi to Visit Assam for Key India-Japan Business Summit
summary: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is scheduled to visit Assam along with a delegation of Japanese business leaders for a significant India-Japan bilateral business summit. The visit underscores the growing India-Japan strategic and economic partnership, with investment in Northeast India being a key focus area. Assam has been increasingly positioned as a hub for Southeast and East Asian connectivity under India's Act East Policy.
exam_angle: Relevant for Act East Policy, India-Japan special strategic and global partnership, and investment in Northeast India; connects to GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Infrastructure/Economy).
key_facts: Visitor: Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi
Location: Assam
Purpose: India-Japan Business Summit with business delegation
Policy context: India's Act East Policy; India-Japan Special Strategic and Global Partnership

title: Reliance AGM 2026: Mukesh Ambani Announces AI-First Strategy, Calls for India to Lead Globally in AI
summary: At Reliance Industries' Annual General Meeting (AGM) 2026, Chairman Mukesh Ambani unveiled an AI-first strategy for the conglomerate, emphasising that India must emerge as a global leader in artificial intelligence. The announcements covered investments in AI infrastructure, data centres, and integration of AI across Reliance's businesses including Jio and retail. Ambani positioned India's large talent pool and data base as strategic assets in the global AI race.
exam_angle: Relevant for India's digital economy ambitions, AI policy, and private sector role in technology leadership; connects to GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech/Economy).
key_facts: Event: Reliance Industries AGM 2026
Announced by: Mukesh Ambani, Chairman, Reliance Industries
Focus: AI-first strategy; AI infrastructure, data centres
India's aim: Global leadership in Artificial Intelligence

title: Nobel Laureate John Jumper Leaves Google DeepMind for Anthropic
summary: John Jumper, the Nobel Prize-winning scientist credited with developing AlphaFold — the AI system that predicted protein structures — has resigned from Google DeepMind to join Anthropic, an AI safety company. Jumper was awarded the Nobel Prize in Chemistry 2024 for his work on protein structure prediction using AI. His move to Anthropic, known for its focus on AI safety and its Claude AI models, signals growing competition in the frontier AI space.
exam_angle: Relevant for persons in news; connects to AI governance, Nobel Prize in Chemistry 2024, and the global AI safety debate important for GS Paper 3.
key_facts: Person: John Jumper — Nobel Prize in Chemistry 2024 winner
Previous organisation: Google DeepMind
New organisation: Anthropic (AI safety company)
Achievement: Co-developed AlphaFold for AI-based protein structure prediction

title: Chinese AI Models Dominate Global Top 10 by Token Usage, Says Report
summary: A new report reveals that Chinese artificial intelligence models now account for the majority share among the global top 10 AI models when ranked by token usage. This signals the rapid rise of Chinese AI capabilities and their adoption globally, challenging the dominance of American AI companies such as OpenAI and Anthropic. The development has significant implications for global AI governance, data sovereignty, and technology competition.
exam_angle: Relevant for global AI competition, India's AI strategy, and technology geopolitics; connects to GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech) and GS Paper 2 (IR).
key_facts: Finding: Chinese AI models hold majority share in global top 10 by token usage
Significance: Challenges US dominance in frontier AI
Implication: Raises concerns about AI governance, data security, and geopolitical technology competition
Source: Report cited by NDTV Profit

title: Anthropic AI Blackout Episode Sparks Debate on Need for Global AI Governance
summary: A temporary blackout or outage at Anthropic, a leading frontier AI company, has reignited global debate about the need for robust international governance frameworks for advanced AI systems. Commentators and experts argue that as AI becomes critical infrastructure, unilateral failures by private companies can have global consequences. The episode underscores calls for multilateral agreements and regulatory bodies similar to those governing nuclear or aviation sectors.
exam_angle: Relevant for AI governance debates, international regulatory frameworks, and India's position in global AI policy discussions; connects to GS Paper 2 (IR/Governance) and GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech).
key_facts: Company involved: Anthropic (frontier AI company, USA)
Issue: AI system blackout/outage highlighting infrastructure risks
Demand: Global governance framework for frontier AI systems
Analogy drawn: Governance akin to nuclear or aviation international regulatory bodies

title: Modi Government Launches Initiative to Reverse India's Brain Drain
summary: The Modi government has announced an initiative aimed at reversing India's brain drain by incentivising highly skilled Indian professionals and scientists working abroad to return and contribute to India's technological and economic growth. The policy involves improving research ecosystems, funding, and career opportunities within India, particularly in AI, biotech, and semiconductor sectors. This connects to broader goals of building an innovation ecosystem domestically.
exam_angle: Relevant for India's human capital policy, R&D investment, and innovation ecosystem; connects to GS Paper 2 (Governance) and GS Paper 3 (Economy/Sci-Tech).
key_facts: Launched by: Government of India (Modi government)
Objective: Reverse brain drain; attract Indian diaspora professionals back to India
Focus sectors: AI, biotech, semiconductors, research
Mechanism: Improved research funding, career opportunities, ecosystem building

title: Sports Ministry to Set Up Task Force for Revival of Formula 1 in India
summary: India's Sports Minister has announced the formation of a task force to explore the revival of Formula 1 (F1) motor racing in India. India last hosted an F1 Grand Prix at the Buddh International Circuit in Greater Noida, Uttar Pradesh, from 2011 to 2013, before losing its place on the F1 calendar. The task force will examine logistical, infrastructural, and financial requirements for India to re-enter the F1 race calendar.
exam_angle: Relevant for sports infrastructure policy, India's soft power through global sporting events, and related government initiatives; connects to GS Paper 2 (Governance/Schemes).
key_facts: Announced by: India's Sports Minister
Initiative: Task force for revival of F1 Grand Prix in India
Previous venue: Buddh International Circuit, Greater Noida, Uttar Pradesh
India hosted F1: 2011–2013 (Indian Grand Prix)

title: India's Economy Shows Resilience Against West Asia Geopolitical Shock
summary: Analysis by The Indian Express highlights how India's economy successfully weathered the economic shock from the West Asia (Middle East) conflict, which had threatened to spike global oil prices and disrupt remittance flows. India's diversified energy sourcing strategy, including increased imports from Russia, helped cushion the impact of potential supply disruptions. Forex reserves remained robust and the rupee showed relative stability during the period of geopolitical stress.
exam_angle: Relevant for India's energy security, economic resilience, external sector management, and impact of geopolitical events on India's economy; GS Paper 3 (Economy).
key_facts: Threat: West Asia conflict risking oil price spike and remittance disruption
India's response: Diversified energy imports including from Russia
Outcome: Forex reserves stable; rupee relatively resilient
Key factor: India's energy sourcing flexibility post-Ukraine conflict

title: Karnataka Appoints Two New Additional Advocate Generals in Supreme Court
summary: The Karnataka government has appointed Kunal Vajani and Omar Hoda as Additional Advocate Generals to represent the state in the Supreme Court of India. The Advocate General is the highest law officer of a state, and Additional Advocate Generals assist in handling the state's litigation in higher courts. Such appointments are made under Article 165 of the Constitution which provides for the office of Advocate General for each state.
exam_angle: Connects to constitutional provisions under Article 165 (Advocate General of State); relevant for questions on state legal officers and constitutional functionaries.
key_facts: State: Karnataka
Appointed: Kunal Vajani and Omar Hoda as Additional Advocate Generals
Court: Supreme Court of India
Constitutional provision: Article 165 — Advocate General for the State
